domain:
In the context of a theater organization, various plays are performed in one or more theaters, each identified by its own location, for example "The Northwest Theater". A play may have multiple scheduled sessions, and each session occurs on a scheduled date. Plays are identified by a unique name and is either a musical or an opera. Furthermore, each play may take place during the day or at night.

Each play is written by one or more authors, who are uniquely identified by name. Plays involve participants each also identified by name, and the same participant may act in multiple plays. For each play session, the starting time and an ending time is recorded, and participants can act in one or more sessions. Both authors and participant can be the same person, who may write a play and participate in its performance.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Play in the relationship with Session?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a session can exist without any associated play.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a session must have at least one associated play.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Session can exist without Play.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,Session must have at least one Play.
questions.selected: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,Session must have at least one Play.

questions.question:
We know that each theater has a location. When we think about location, is it just a simple piece of information, like a line of text? 
Or is location made up of different parts that might be important?
For example:
1. For each location, the detail information is saved separately, so users can filter theater by detail.
2. The location is a single piece of text. This works well when we just need to show the location.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: The Location is a separate class with its own attributes.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Model location as a simple representation in Theater.
questions.selected: Option 2: Model location as a simple representation in Theater.
questions.llm_raw_response:
Option 2: Model location as a simple representation in Theater.

The domain description does not provide any details about the need for individual location properties or attributes, suggesting that a simple representation is sufficient for the context of the theater organization.

questions.question:
When thinking about the location of theaters, are there only specific, predefined values it can have, or can it be any text?
For example:
1. The location can be any text value that users want to enter.
2. The location must be one of these specific values: the northwest theater.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: String attribute that allows any text value for location.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Enumeration location with predefined values: literal 1, literal 2 and literal 3.
questions.selected: Option 1: The location can be any text value.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Play in the relationship with Theater?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a theater can exist without any associated play.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a theater must have at least one associated play.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Theater can exist without Play.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, Theater must have at least one Play.
questions.selected: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Theater can exist without Play.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Theater in the relationship with Play?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a play can exist without any associated theater.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a play must have at least one associated theater.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Play can exist without Theater.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, Play must have at least one Theater.
questions.selected: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, Play must have at least one Theater.

questions.question:
Is person a relevant domain concept that should be included in the model?

For example:
1. Person is a relevant concept in this domain and should be represented in the model.
2. Person does not correspond to any relevant domain concept and should be removed from the model.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Keep person, it is a relevant domain concept.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Remove person, it is not relevant.
questions.selected: Option 1: Keep person, it is a relevant domain concept.

questions.question:
Should Session be modeled as an association class linking Play and Theater, or as a regular class with two separate associations?

For example:
1. Regular class: Session is a class that has links to Play and Theater. In this case multiple sessions can exist for every Play and Theater.
2. Association class: Session exists only as part of the relationship between Play and Theater. A session cannot exist without the link between Play and Theater.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Association class, Session as association class linking Play and Theater.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Regular class, Session as separate class with two associations.
questions.selected: Option 1: Association class, Session as association class linking Play and Theater.

questions.question:
Should Person be modeled as an abstract class or as a concrete class?
Consider whether Person can be instantiated directly, or only through its subclasses (Author or Participant).

For example:
1. Concrete class  Person ithat can be instantiated. Class people can exist independently.
2. Abstract class  Person that cannot be instantiated. Every person must be a specific type author or participant.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Concrete class Person that can be instantiated directly.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Abstract class Person, where only the subclasses can be instantiated.
questions.selected: Option 2: Abstract class Person, where only the subclasses can be instantiated.
